The Deterrence Deficit names what the empirical literature has long established but Indian child protection policy has not yet systematically absorbed: that severity-based punitive frameworks, however necessary, demonstrably fail to arrest offending trajectories when deployed without complementary preventive educational infrastructure. Regulatory Realism articulates the policy philosophy that follows from the developmental criminological tradition — from Sutherland through Wikström — that the conditions of childhood are not fixed and that their educational regulation is a legitimate and underutilized instrument of child protection. The empirical component of this inquiry, drawn from N=108 stakeholders across Bhopal Division's five districts, provides ground-level validation for these constructs: among practitioners, the deterrent efficacy of police action achieved only a 56 per cent endorsement — a respondent-reported perception consistent with what Nagin (1998) and Doob and Webster (2003) have established about severity-based deterrence across jurisdictions. Together, these three constructs provide the criminological foundation for the Integrated Bio-Ethical Literacy (IBL) Model — not a departure from the discipline's intellectual tradition, but its extension into the domain of primary prevention that Lombroso raised but could not answer and that Sutherland opened but did not close.

title: Ginagnotología: la producción estructural de ignorancia en la salud de las mujeres / Gynagnotology: The Structural Production of Ignorance in Women’s Health
abstract: RESUMEN En las últimas décadas, la literatura científica ha documentado de forma consistente la presencia de sesgos de sexo y género en la producción y aplicación del conocimiento biomédico, generando lagunas relevantes en la salud de las mujeres. Estas desigualdades no pueden explicarse únicamente mediante sesgos individuales, errores metodológicos o falta de evidencia, sino que responden a dinámicas estructurales que producen y organizan ignorancia de manera sistemática. Este trabajo introduce y desarrolla el concepto de ginagnotología, propuesto por la autora, como un campo analítico dentro de la agnotología que examina cómo el sesgo de sexo y género opera como un mecanismo estructural de producción, organización y legitimación de la ignorancia en el sistema biomédico. A partir de este concepto, se propone un marco triádico que articula tres niveles analíticos interrelacionados: ginagnotología (nivel epistemológico), ginopia médica (nivel clínico) y morbilidad diferencial (nivel epidemiológico), permitiendo conectar la producción de ignorancia con sus manifestaciones en la práctica clínica y sus efectos en las desigualdades en salud. La ginagnotología ofrece así un marco conceptual para convertir la ignorancia estructurada en objeto de estudio crítico y orientar su transformación, abriendo vías hacia una biomedicina más rigurosa y equitativa. ABSTRACT In recent decades, scientific literature has consistently documented the presence of sex and gender biases in the production and application of biomedical knowledge, generating significant gaps in women’s health. These inequalities cannot be explained solely by individual biases, methodological errors, or lack of evidence, but rather reflect structural dynamics that systematically produce and organize ignorance. This paper introduces and develops the concept of gynagnotology, proposed by the author, as an analytical field within agnotology that examines how sex and gender bias operates as a structural mechanism for the production, organization, and legitimation of ignorance in the biomedical system. Building on this concept, a triadic framework is proposed that articulates three interrelated analytical levels: gynagnotology (epistemological level), medical gynopia (clinical level), and differential morbidity (epidemiological level), enabling the connection between the production of ignorance, its manifestation in clinical practice, and its effects on health inequalities. Gynagnotology thus provides a conceptual framework to turn structured ignorance into an object of critical inquiry and to guide its transformation, opening pathways toward a more rigorous and equitable biomedicine.

title: Metodologi Penelitian Adaptif di Era Digital
abstract: Buku ini berfokus pada pemahaman mendalam tentang konsep metodologi yang tidak hanya bersifat prosedural, tetapi juga reflektif dan inovatif. Pembaca akan diajak menelusuri bagaimana Artificial Intelligence (AI), digitalisasi data, dan konektivitas global telah mengubah lanskap penelitian modern. Salah satu gagasan penting yang diperkenalkan dalam buku ini adalah Model F.A.D.L.I (Formulate, Analyze, Design, and Improve) sebuah pendekatan penelitian dan pengembangan (Research and Development Model) yang dirancang untuk menuntun peneliti agar mampu merumuskan ide, menganalisis secara sistematis, mendesain solusi, dan melakukan perbaikan berkelanjutan. Model ini diharapkan dapat menjadi inspirasi dan rujukan baru bagi mahasiswa, dosen, serta peneliti yang ingin beradaptasi dengan era digital tanpa kehilangan esensi keilmuan.

title: "I'd Rather Have Milk Tea": Reproductive Refusal, Platform Divergence, and the Gendered Architecture of Fertility Discourse on Chinese Social Media--An AI-Assisted Topic Modeling Study
authors: Frank Tian-fang Ye, GAO Xiaozi, Mac Zewei Ma
abstract: China's total fertility rate has fallen to approximately 0.98, the lowest since 1949, yet the discursive dimensions of this decline remain underexplored. This study examines how young Chinese women articulate fertility attitudes across two platforms with contrasting affordances: Weibo, oriented toward public debate, and Xiaohongshu (RedNote), oriented toward lifestyle and embodied experience. Using BERTopic neural topic modelling on 495,893 Weibo posts (2023 to 2025) and 222 viral Xiaohongshu posts, we identify 164 Weibo topics that we organise into seven meta-themes spanning reproductive refusal, structural constraints, and embodied experience. Temporal analysis reveals that reproductive autonomy anxiety dominated Weibo discourse for 31 consecutive months, while a "blind box" metaphor for childbearing rose steadily in prominence. Cross-platform comparison shows that the same generation processes fertility politically on Weibo, through feminist critique and structural analysis, but embodies it on Xiaohongshu through pregnancy, postpartum, and lifestyle frames. We argue that this divergence reflects the interaction of platform affordances, algorithmic curation, and content moderation rather than distinct populations. The persistence of reproductive refusal signals deep tensions between state pronatalism and young women's lived experience of inadequate support for motherhood.

title: A Comparison of Process Tracing and Congruence Analysis for the Evaluation of a Population Health Intervention
authors: Emma Carey, David Ogilvie, Kate Garrott, Richard Patterson, Olivia Alliott, Jenna Panter
abstract: Mixed methods evaluations of population health interventions are often comprised of diverse components which must be integrated to provide holistic insight into effects and their causes. Theory-based methods can be used to support this integration, but research into how to select which method to use is limited. Traffic restriction schemes that limit motor vehicle access to the roads outside of schools in the UK have recently proliferated in the UK. We explore how two theory-based methods – process tracing (theory testing) and congruence analysis – could be used to evaluate such schemes. We use hypothetical data to illustrate how each method could be applied. In our case, congruence analysis was more suitable because we sought to evaluate multiple possible pathways by which schemes might influence travel. Theory-testing process tracing would be more suited to research with narrower hypotheses. Combining approaches may also help to both identify and examine key causal pathways.
